description_long: This shotgun features a rugged, industrial design with exposed mechanical components and a mix of metal and wood materials. The receiver and barrel assembly are constructed from dark, weathered metal, with visible wiring and tubing running along the sides, suggesting a custom or improvised build. The stock is a simple, angular wooden piece with a worn finish, and the grip area is reinforced with additional metal bracing. The barrel is relatively short and thick, with a cylindrical muzzle and a prominent front sight. Several mechanical parts, including a large cylindrical chamber and a visible trigger mechanism, are integrated into the body. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with a focus on function over aesthetics, making it suitable for urban environments where durability and reliability are prioritized.
description: A rugged, industrial shotgun with exposed metal components, visible wiring, and a worn wooden stock. Its utilitarian design features a short, thick barrel and a reinforced grip.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle, front sight, and the alignment of the barrel with visible mechanical details.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the full length of the shotgun, exposed wiring, cylindrical chamber, and the wooden stock with metal bracing.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the stock, grip, and the back of the receiver, with the wiring and mechanical parts partially visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the shotgun's mechanical complexity, wiring, and the worn finish of the wooden stock.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel with a thick, cylindrical profile and a prominent front sight; no ribbing or venting visible
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with a simple, unreinforced design
